policy.description: Blocking line-coverage ratchets. Update measured counts in the same reviewed change whenever scope or coverage advances; never lower a floor without an explicit, justified scope change.

tolerance.rationale: Theme Studio Canopy-surface integration in issue #393. Repeated clean local Node 22/V8 coverage runs on 2026-07-21 each passed all 1594 tests over the identical complete 2288-line core scope and measured 1963 covered lines (85.795%). Relative to the prior 1932/2253 baseline, the legitimate 35-line scope increase adds identity-owned tag-lane attachment and cleanup, semantic notification visibility states, and native-indicator compatibility through tag lanes. Focused tests cover same-corner reuse, stable position metadata, identity ownership, empty-lane cleanup, unknown-position fallback, stock RTL exits, visibility transitions, and the nested native-indicator selector. Covered lines advance by 31 and total core coverage improves from 85.752% to 85.795%. The existing one-line tolerance is unchanged and permits only the previously reproduced negligible instrumentation variance; coverage-baseline.test.js retains exact below-floor, above-baseline, scope-change, and broad-tolerance negative boundaries.

Theme Studio operational surfaces in issue #394. Two clean local .NET 10.0.9/Coverlet runs on 2026-07-21 each passed all 1831 tests over the identical complete 29213-line assembly scope and measured 20777 covered lines. Relative to the prior 20771/29207 baseline, the legitimate six-line scope increase registers the same-origin embedded operational stylesheet and every new instrumented manifest line is covered by the asset-cache assembly test; total assembly coverage improves from 71.117% to 71.122%. The existing four-line low-side tolerance is unchanged and remains far below the policy maximum; coverage-baseline.test.js retains the exact scope-drift, regression, stale-baseline, and broad-tolerance negative boundaries.
